source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a bolted multi-body sheet-metal L-bracket assembly consisting of: (A) one cold-rolled steel L-bracket formed from 1.6 mm SPCC/DC01 blank (yield 210 MPa, UTS 350 MPa, 2.4 mm inside bend radius); (B) one self-clinching #6-32 UNC nut insert (steel/stainless) press-installed into a pre-punched 4.19 mm (+0.08/-0.00) hole in the host sheet; (C) one #6-32 grade 5 screw that threads into the insert during service; (D) the host-sheet plastically-flowed region around the insert (represented as a distinct cohesive/contact zone in the FEM that models the clinch engagement).

The host sheet is 1.6 mm cold-rolled steel (SPCC / DC01). The L-bracket has leg A 80 x 50 mm with two mounting slots (6 x 12 mm on 60 mm centerline spacing) and leg B 60 x 50 mm carrying the self-clinching insert at its centroid. The insert must maintain minimum edge distance >= 6.5 mm (per minimum edge-distance spec for #6-32 thread).

Representative catalog 2024 values for the #6-32 UNC self-clinching insert installed in low-carbon steel host sheet (HRB 80 max per install spec): minimum installation force ~ 13.3 kN (3,000 lbf), minimum pull-out force 2.45 kN (550 lbf), minimum torque-out 2.7 N*m (24 in*lbf).

Service loads applied through the #6-32 grade 5 screw threaded into the insert: LC1 axial pull-out load 2.45 kN (full rated) normal to leg B; LC2 installation torque reaction 2.7 N*m on the insert-to-sheet interface; LC3 combined shear 500 N along leg B plus 1 kN normal to leg B.

Pass/fail on the assembled model: (R1) at LC1 rated pull-out, max principal stress in the host sheet around the insert <= UTS / 1.25 = 280 MPa (self-clinching-insert design-guide SF 1.25 against sheet tear-out at rated pull-out); (R2) at LC2 rated torque-out, host-sheet von Mises around the insert <= yield / 1.0 = 210 MPa on the first torque cycle (insert clinch friction governs, catalog practice); (R3) L-bracket max von Mises under LC3 <= yield / 1.5 = 140 MPa (general sheet-metal SF); (R4) L-bracket deflection at the insert location under LC3 <= 0.5 mm (to preserve screw preload integrity); (R5) host-sheet minimum edge-distance 6.5 mm to any free edge and 9.5 mm between adjacent self-clinching insert hole centerlines (catalog constraint); (R6) host hole diameter tolerance 4.19 +0.08/-0.00 mm (catalog install spec); (R_asm1) clinch engagement DCR (contact pressure at the knurl/undercut of the insert vs the plastically-flowed host sheet) <= 1.0 under LC1.

The bracket is manufactured by laser-cutting a flat blank, punching the 4.19 mm insert hole, V-die forming to the L shape, and pressing the insert on an arbor press. The final FEM check runs on the fully assembled model (L-bracket + insert + screw + clinch engagement contact zone), with nonlinear contact and friction at the insert knurl/undercut. Per-part stress is reported for each part family but assembly-level passing requires all interface DCRs and tear-out/torque-out limits to hold simultaneously.

Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the formed L-bracket assembly with insert and screw as distinct bodies + flat-pattern DXF; report listing sheet max principal stress at LC1, von Mises at LC2, bracket max von Mises + deflection at LC3, clinch engagement DCR, per-part max stress, and a pass/fail line for edge-distance and hole-diameter compliance.
short_prompt:
Design a bolted multi-body sheet-metal L-bracket assembly with a self-clinching #6-32 UNC nut insert press-installed into a 1.6 mm SPCC/DC01 host sheet (yield 210 MPa, UTS 350 MPa, HRB 80 max), joined at service by a #6-32 grade 5 screw. The L-bracket has 2.4 mm inside bend radius, leg A 80 x 50 mm with two 6 x 12 mm slots on 60 mm centerlines, leg B 60 x 50 mm carrying the insert at its centroid. Host-sheet insert hole 4.19 +0.08/-0.00 mm; edge distance >= 6.5 mm.

Catalog 2024 values: install force ~ 13.3 kN (3000 lbf); pull-out 2.45 kN (550 lbf); torque-out 2.7 N*m (24 in*lbf).

Load cases.
- LC1 pull-out rated: 2.45 kN axial normal to leg B.
- LC2 torque-out rated: 2.7 N*m on the insert-to-sheet interface.
- LC3 combined service: 500 N shear along leg B + 1 kN normal.

Pass limits: host sheet max principal at LC1 <= UTS/1.25 = 280 MPa; host sheet von Mises at LC2 <= 210 MPa; bracket von Mises at LC3 <= 210/1.5 = 140 MPa; bracket deflection at insert under LC3 <= 0.5 mm.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Bend allowance and K-factor computation for the formed L is deterministic CAD/CAM, not FEA.
Self-clinching insert install force curves are tabulated, not simulated.

prompt.title: Sheet-metal L-bracket with self-clinching nut insert (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Multi-body clinch-insert joint: 1.6 mm SPCC/DC01 L-bracket + #6-32 self-clinching nut insert + #6-32 grade 5 screw + clinch engagement contact zone. Final FEM check runs on the assembled model only with nonlinear contact at the clinch knurl.

parts.name: sheet_metal_L_bracket
parts.role: primary L-formed host sheet that receives the self-clinching insert and carries service load to the mounting slots

parts.name: self_clinching_insert
parts.role: #6-32 UNC self-clinching nut insert press-installed in leg B

parts.name: machine_screw
parts.role: #6-32 grade 5 screw that threads into the insert during service and delivers pull-out / torque-out / combined load

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: approximately 80 x 60 x 50 mm (formed L outer envelope)
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: Leg A in the XY plane; leg B rotated 90 deg about the bend line

boundary_conditions.insert_interface: clinch contact with radial interference, friction + form locking
boundary_conditions.inter_part_fastener_stiffness: nonlinear contact between insert knurl/undercut and the plastically-flowed host sheet; friction coefficient per insert install guide

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: UTS / 1.25 = 350 / 1.25 (self-clinching-insert design-guide tear-out SF at rated pull-out)
contact pressure at the insert knurl/undercut vs the plastically-flowed host sheet, normalized by catalog retention

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: TET10 or HEX20 with elasto-plastic host sheet
analysis_types: nonlinear static with contact + friction at clinch

interfaces.load_path: insert knurl/undercut engages plastically-flowed host sheet; pull-out resisted by form lock + friction
screw thread engaged with insert internal thread; transmits service tension/shear

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: A clinch-insert joint is fundamentally a three-body interaction (host sheet + insert + screw) where host-sheet tear-out governs pull-out; a single-body model cannot represent the plastic clinch engagement.
notes.speculation_caveats: The screw body (C) is speculated because the original prompt frames the loads as applied through a #6-32 grade 5 screw. Part-family counts are the minimum required to form the joint.
